stories.title: CPSC Emergency Warning — Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinet (Stop Use + Destroy)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: 🚨 CPSC WARNING: Stop using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets — strangulation + fall hazard. ~250 sold on Amazon 2025, ~$40. Cut cover in half before disposal. Company refused recall. Do not resell. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Tuyedoqe bassinet company refused a recall, so CPSC issued a Notice of Violation. Consumer awareness is the only protection. Stop use and destroy it — cut the cover in half first. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Emergency Warning: Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets — Company Refused Recall

research.facts:
Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinet — CPSC Emergency Warning
**Sources:**
- CPSC.gov official warning page: "CPSC Warns Consumers to Immediately Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Strangulation and Fall Hazards" — CONFIRMED
**Freshness justification:** Not previously covered in Parenting story history. CPSC emergency consumer warning (not a standard recall — company refused to cooperate). Approximately 250 units sold on Amazon April-October 2025.
**Verified facts (HIGH — CPSC.gov official):**
- ~250 units sold on Amazon.com, April–October 2025, ~$40
- Hazards: restraint system poses strangulation risk; sides too low (fall/entrapment); no stand means unsafe on elevated surfaces
- Company refused recall → CPSC issued Notice of Violation (this is unusual and newsworthy)
- Action: Immediately stop use. Remove foam padding. Cut cover in half. Dispose both pieces. Do NOT resell or donate. Do NOT use as storage.
**Verified facts (HIGH — CPSC.gov):**
- This is a Notice of Violation, not a standard recall — important distinction for article

stories.title: COS Childcare Desert — 47K Kids Under 5, Only 18.4K Spots; June 2026 Fix Coming

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: COS has 47,000 children under age 5 and only 18,400 licensed childcare spots — a gap of nearly 29,000 seats. A June 2026 city ordinance aims to fix zoning rules that made the shortage worse. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: A UCCS study: 26% of COS parents who would otherwise work stay home because childcare isn't available. The city's proposed ordinance fix targets June 2026. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: COS Has a Childcare Crisis: 47,000 Kids, 18,400 Spots, and a City Trying to Fix What Zoning Broke

research.facts:
COS Childcare Desert — 47K Kids, 18.4K Spots
**Sources:**
- KOAA: "Colorado Springs considers zoning changes to expand access to childcare"
- KKTV: "'We have a big childcare crisis' — Jan. 31, 2026"
- KRDO: "City proposes solution to 'childcare desert' — Feb. 4, 2026"
- City of Colorado Springs: coloradosprings.gov/ChildcareOrdinanceUpdate
- Colorado Springs Gazette: Feb. 14, 2026 coverage
**Freshness justification:** The childcare ordinance update is targeting June 2026 — approximately 8 weeks from now. This is actionable near-term news. Not previously covered in Parenting story history.
**Verified facts (HIGH — 4+ sources):**
- 47,000 children under age 5 in COS; 18,400 licensed spots; ~29,000 seat gap
- UCCS study: 26% of parents who would otherwise work stay home due to childcare unavailability
- Southeast COS and Fountain-Fort Carson face worst shortage
- 2023 Unified Development Code inadvertently banned new in-home and church-based childcare
- Childcare Ordinance Update targeting June 2026 implementation
**Verified facts (MEDIUM — 1 source each):**
- Kids Strong Start 10-week awareness campaign launched January 2026 by Joint Initiatives for Youth & Families and Family Friendly Initiative (KOAA)
- City has held public input meetings on the ordinance update (Gazette)

stories.title: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Turns 100 — Dog Days This Friday, Giraffe Center This Summer

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: 🐾 Dog Days at Cheyenne Mountain Zoo is THIS FRIDAY, April 10 — bring the family and the dog. CMZoo is celebrating its 100th year in 2026, just named No. 2 zoo in the U.S. by USA Today readers. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Turns 100: Dog Days This Friday, New Giraffe Center Coming This Summer

research.facts:
Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Centennial
**Sources:**
- FOX21: "2026 marks 100 years of Cheyenne Mountain Zoo"
- KRDO: "Cheyenne Mountain Zoo history exhibit opens" — March 18, 2026
- Denver Gazette: "Colorado zoo voted as 2nd best zoo in U.S. in USA Today awards" — March 18, 2026
- CMZoo official events page: cmzoo.org/events-programs/
**Freshness justification:** Dog Days is FRIDAY April 10 — immediate hook. CMZoo centennial year is ongoing. New giraffe center opening this summer is forward-looking. "No. 2 zoo in U.S." per USA Today 2026 is recent. Not previously covered in April 2026 story history (April 7 was Mueller State Park, April 6 was Garden of the Gods).
**Verified facts (HIGH — 3+ sources):**
- CMZoo 2026 is 100th anniversary year
- Dog Days events April 10 and April 24 (dogs welcome, all-day)
- New history exhibit at Penrose Heritage Museum opened March 2026
- New International Center for the Care and Conservation of Giraffe: 12,000 sq ft, 25% expanded outdoor space, opening summer 2026
- USA Today readers poll: No. 2 zoo in U.S. (2026)

article.headline: Spring Programs at Bear Creek Nature Center: Birding, Bird Banding, and Homeschool Days This April

research.facts:
Bear Creek Nature Center April Programs
**Sources:**
- El Paso County Parks official site: communityservices.elpasoco.com/nature-centers/bear-creek-nature-center/
**Freshness justification:** Programs start April 9 (tomorrow) — immediate hook. Not previously covered in Parenting story history.
**Verified facts (MEDIUM — 1 official source):**
- April 9 & 11: Intermediate birding class with Aiken Audubon, $10/person, $8/member, ages 14+
- April 18, 7–9 AM: Bird banding station with CSU-Pueblo researchers, $5/person, ages 8+
- April 23, 1–3:30 PM: Homeschool programs (multi-age), $4/person, pre-registration required
- Location: Bear Creek Nature Center, 245 Bear Creek Road, Colorado Springs
**Note:** MEDIUM confidence because single official source. All prices and times are from El Paso County Parks official programming page. Families should confirm by calling the center before attending.

stories.title: CPSC Recalls — Olympia Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagon + Uuoeebb Infant Walkers

x_posts.label: Post 5-1
x_posts.text: 🚨 RECALL: Olympia Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons — gap between tray and sidewall can trap a child's head, no restraint system. Stop use, contact Olympia for remedy. Check cpsc.gov for model details. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 5-2
x_posts.text: 🚨 RECALL: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers (Amazon/BaoD) — can pass through doorways + fail at step edges. Leg openings allow infant to slip until head is entrapped. Stop use. Full details at cpsc.gov. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: 2 CPSC Recalls Before Spring: Olympia Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons and Uuoeebb Infant Walkers

research.facts:
Olympia Pack-N-Stroll + Uuoeebb Infant Walker Recalls
**Sources:**
- CPSC.gov: "Olympia Tools International Recalls Pack-N-Stroll Utility Wagons Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Entrapment and Falls"
- CPSC.gov: "Uuoeebb Infant Walkers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Fall and Entrapment Hazards — Violate Mandatory Standard for Infant Walkers — Sold on Amazon by BaoD"
**Freshness justification:** Both are new CPSC recalls not previously covered in Parenting story history. Wagon recalls are timely (spring outdoor season). Walker recalls always high-priority.
**Verified facts (HIGH — CPSC.gov official):**
- Olympia Pack-N-Stroll: gap between organizational tray and sidewall can entrap child's head; no restraint system. Violates mandatory stroller standard. Contact Olympia for remedy.
- Uuoeebb Infant Walkers: can pass through standard doorways; fail to stop at step edges (fall hazard); leg openings allow infant to slip, head can become entrapped. Violates mandatory standard for infant walkers. Sold on Amazon by BaoD. Contact seller for remedy.
**Note:** Exact unit counts and sale prices not confirmed from available search data — will frame conservatively ("sold on Amazon") without specifying unit counts that are not confirmed.
